§6Solvent§f

§aCorrosive chemical feedstock — essential for MK.III upgrades, versatile circuits, and bedrock ore processing!§f

Solvent is a corrosive, flammable liquid produced by mixing §bAromatic Hydrocarbons§f and §bDesulfurized Naphtha§f in the §bIndustrial Mixer§f. It provides §e100 kTU§f per bucket but its real value lies in advanced crafting.

§6Production§f:
§b• Industrial Mixer§f: §e500mB§f Aromatic Hydrocarbons + §e500mB§f Desulfurized Naphtha → §e1,000mB§f Solvent.

§6Uses§f:
§b• Effectiveness Upgrade Mk.III§f (Soldering Station): §e500mB§f Solvent + Capacitors + Microchips + Rubber.
§b• Versatile Circuit Board§f (Soldering Station): §e1,000mB§f Solvent + advanced components.
§b• Ore Acidizer§f: Used to dissolve bedrock ore components.

§eTip§f: Solvent bridges the gap between basic petroleum processing and §bHPS§f (High-Performance Solvent). You need Desulfurized Naphtha, which requires the Hydrotreater. Set up a dedicated Industrial Mixer feeding into your circuit production line!
